text: here ?” Her question was so unexpected that he wasn’t sure how to process it. Hello or what’s going on would have been easier to respond to . “What are you talking about?” He lowered the ladder. The wood groaned as he mounted it, boards flexing enough beneath his weight that he had to adjust his stabilizers . Lara backed away, allowing him enough space to climb onto the dusty attic floor. Her footprints were everywhere, in a meandering trail from one side of the attic to the other, and back again. It was eighteen degrees warmer up here than in the hallway below. Her damp hair was plastered to her face, and a sheen of perspiration covered her skin . “You’re hurt,” he said, raising a hand to brush her hair away from the dried blood at her temple . “It’s nothing.” She ducked out of his reach and pulled more hair over the wound. “The ladder had the upper hand on me.” One corner of her mouth lifted, and she snickered. “ Get it ?” Ronin dropped his hand to his side. Internally, he combed his stored data for information about human head injuries. Were nonsensical ramblings and statements without context sign of deeper trauma ? “I…got everything I intended at the Market ?” “Wow. Okay. We’ll add no appreciation for witty humor to the list. Anyway—wait. What happened to your hands ?” She moved around to his side, staring at his hands. He lifted them, turning his palms toward the ceiling, and inspected the new skin. It was strange to see them this way again, after so long. They were undoubtedly his hands, but seemed somehow out of place . “Went to the Clinic and had them reskinned. Been a long time since I’ve done it. I figured it was overdue .” “You can do that? Like, grow new skin ?” Did she realize how close to him she was moving in her curiosity ? “It’s synthesized from various materials. Not grown .” She looked into his optics, her brows furrowed. “Why don’t the gearheads do it , then ?” “Because they don’t want to be mistaken for humans .” “Yeah. Because we’re so horrible.” She rolled her eyes and walked toward the window. “Come look at this .” Not sure how to respond to her comment, he followed her to the far end of the attic. He took in the scene — the bookcase full of jars, the blankets and clothing on the floor, the table and chair — as Lara knelt . She picked up a book with a brown leather cover. “I can’t read it, but someone wrote this.” She held it out to him . “Every book was written by someone,” Ronin replied. He took the book, running his fingertips over the textured surface . “No, smart ass. This one was written by hand . Not like the other ones. And this,” she gestured to the blankets, “was where he slept .” His optics followed her gesture before returning to the book. Carefully, he opened it. The pages were stiff, but in surprisingly good condition . Those things killed people today. Marched into town and just started killing people. I watched from the window as people were dragged into the park and executed . “And this is what he ate,” Lara said, calling Ronin’s attention back to her. She stood near the shelf, holding a jar with a dark, unidentifiable substance inside it. “Trust me when I say not to open them .” Ronin studied the objects one at a time, cataloguing them in his memory. It was impossible to say how long ago the words had been written, how long ago the writer had lived. Many, many years, certainly . “ He ?” Lara stepped close to him, turning the pages to reveal the photographs tucked in the middle of the book. The top one was a picture of a family, the adult male and female both with dark hair and eyes. The two children resembled them closely . “Pretty sure it was him,” she said, tapping the man’s face . Humans didn’t look like that anymore. It wasn’t that the people in the picture had drastically different features than modern people; the basic human facial structure and typical proportions were unchanged. No, it was the light in their eyes, the genuine smiles on their faces . The people in the photo were happy, healthy, alive . “And he had a great view,” Lara said from the window. Outside, the sunlight sparkled on the surface of the pond and cast golden halos around the treetops . I watched from the window as people were dragged into the park and executed . “Come away from there,” Ronin said, tone sharp even to his own receptors . “Why?” she asked, eyes narrowed on him . “There’s food downstairs, and you’re likely in need of hydration after spending so much time up here. Go clean yourself up and have something to eat .” An angry crease had formed between her eyebrows, but it vanished once she looked down at herself. Dust clung to her clothing and sweat- dampened skin . “Yeah…guess I didn’t notice. I maybe got a little carried away.” She plucked restlessly at the hem of her skirt before letting it drop . “I found some more clothes for you at the Market,” Ronin said. “They might not fit quite right, but they’ll be better than nothing until we can adjust them.” His boots thumped on the floorboards as he walked to the hatch. “Go on. I’d rather you get down safe before it collapses under me .” “Okay, okay.” With one last glance at the window, she hurried over and climbed down . Ronin’s optics lingered on the scene for a few more seconds before he shifted them to the book in his hand. JOURNAL was barely legible on the front cover. He slipped it into his pocket and headed downstairs .
